one_line_positioning: NIFT-Trained Merchandiser Who Scaled a Women’s Casualwear Category to ₹3.5L Revenue in 2 Months, Ready to Optimize H&M’s Jersey Assortment
narrative_angle: NIFT-trained merchandiser who built a women’s casualwear category from zero, delivering ₹3.5L revenue in 2 months and improving margins from 22% to 35%. While my OTB management has been implicit in these P&L-driven decisions, my rapid learning and commercial instinct will enable me to formalize H&M’s OTB process quickly.
lead_strengths: Built a fashion category from near zero to ₹3.5 lakh revenue in 2 months at SuperK
Improved product margins from 22% to 35% through vendor negotiation
Managed PO/PI coordination across 20+ suppliers with 95% on-time delivery
proof_points_to_emphasize: Managed PO/PI coordination across 20+ suppliers, 95% on-time delivery
Expanded fashion assortment from 5 to 25+ subcategories
Planned assortment for 200+ SKUs with sell-through optimization
Onboarded 12+ regional vendors, negotiated pricing and MOQs to improve margins
things_to_downplay: Explicit OTB management experience – has been implicit within broader category management, not a standalone function
tone_guidance: Outcome-first, numbers-driven, use retail vocabulary (sell-through, MOQ, PLM, markdown), emphasize commercial acumen and speed; avoid AI/tech jargon; mention NIFT early to signal fashion foundation
recruiter_first_impression_target: This candidate has built a fashion category from scratch, improved margins dramatically, and has strong operational chops; though new to H&M’s scale, they bring startup ownership and proven commercial results – worth an interview to discuss OTB approach.
reasoning: Differentiator is building a women’s casualwear category from zero and improving margins by 13pp, with NIFT training. Gap is explicit OTB management, a potential disqualifier, though implicit in P&L-driven assortment planning. Minor gaps in SAP and forecasting. Overall strong match on merchandising fundamentals, so CAREFUL_PUSH stance pre-empting OTB via rapid learning and commercial instinct.
